corpus.title: Ukraine : un remaniement à haut risque ?
corpus.signal_text:
Ukraine : un remaniement à haut risque ?

Le remaniement opéré en Ukraine par le président Volodymyr Zelensky vire à la crise politique. Des centaines de personnes ont manifesté dans les rues pour contester le départ de Mykhaïlo Fedorov du poste de ministre de la Défense.

corpus.title: Why protests are growing in Ukraine over defence leadership and dismissals

Why protests are growing in Ukraine over defence leadership and dismissals

Protests continue as anger grows over President Zelenskyy’s decision to fire the country’s defence minister.

Fedorov out as Ukraine’s defense minister in major government shake-up

Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy dismissed Mykhailo Fedorov as the country's minister of defense after just six months in office.

corpus.title: Ukraine lawmakers approve Koretskyi as new PM amid protests
corpus.signal_text:
Ukraine lawmakers approve Koretskyi as new PM amid protests

It is President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's second Cabinet shakeup in a year. He faces criticism for dismissing reformist Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov.

corpus.title: Foreign Ministry's Ukraine bureau chief: Ukraine's soldiers can't afford to be shaken by political upheaval
corpus.signal_text:
Foreign Ministry's Ukraine bureau chief: Ukraine's soldiers can't afford to be shaken by political upheaval

Although the dismissal of Ukraine's defense minister Mykhailo Fedorov has sparked a wave of protests in the country, frontline soldiers cannot allow political maneuvering to influence them, said Gert Antsu, director of the Estonian Foreign Ministry's Ukraine, Moldova and South Caucasus bureau.

corpus.title: Zelensky Weighs Replacing Syrsky With Drapaty Amid Military Leadership Crisis – Bloomberg
corpus.signal_text:
President Volodymyr Zelensky is considering replacing Commander-in-Chief of the Armed Forces of Ukraine Oleksandr Syrsky with Maj. Gen. Mykhailo Drapaty, the commander of Ukraine’s Joint Forces Command, Bloomberg reported on July 21, citing two sources familiar with the discussions.
One source said Zelensky is likely to appoint Drapaty as Syrsky’s successor, with a decision potentially coming within days. A second source said no final decision has been made and that 11 candidates are being considered.
JOIN US ON TELEGRAM
Follow our coverage of the war on the @Kyivpost_official.
Zelensky’s aide Dmytro Lytvyn declined to comment. Zelensky confirmed he met with Drapaty on Monday, July 20, but did not mention any plans to replace the army chief.
The reported discussions come as Zelensky faces his most serious military leadership crisis since the start of Russia’s full-scale invasion.
Thousands of Ukrainians have taken to the streets in Kyiv, Lviv, Odesa and other cities following the dismissal of Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov on July 15, who had served in the role for only six months but quickly gained public support.
Fedorov said after his dismissal that he had pushed for Syrsky’s removal, accusing the commander-in-chief of blocking military reforms. He also claimed Syrsky had issued Zelensky an “ultimatum” demanding the minister’s dismissal.
Zelensky had previously acknowledged that relations between Fedorov and Syrsky had deteriorated significantly and that the two officials had stopped communicating directly.
Syrsky Breaks Silence, Apologizes to Ousted Defense Minister Fedorov
Syrsky, meanwhile, publicly apologized to Fedorov in a rare personal column published after the dispute between the two officials became public.
He said he was surprised to learn that the minister viewed their relationship as a conflict and denied claims that he had blocked reforms or undermined the defense ministry.
“If I offended someone with something: Mykhailo Albertovych, I’m sorry. I can be tough,” Syrsky wrote, while urging Ukrainians not to turn the dispute into a personal rivalry. “Ukraine is bigger than Fedorov, Syrsky, or any of us.”
Syrsky warned against dividing society into “Fedorov” and “Syrsky” camps, saying Russia would benefit from internal divisions and that the war would be won through “real work, not public drama.”
He also defended the division of responsibilities between the defense ministry and military command, saying the ministry oversees procurement, sup

corpus.title: Syrsky Breaks Silence, Apologizes to Ousted Defense Minister Fedorov
corpus.signal_text:
Syrsky Breaks Silence, Apologizes to Ousted Defense Minister Fedorov

Commander-in-Chief of the Armed Forces of Ukraine (AFU) Oleksandr Syrsky apologized to former Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov, saying he only learned of their conflict after it became public. Fedorov's dismissal last week has sparked protests since, renewing questions about President Volodymyr Zelensky’s military command.

corpus.title: Zelensky reviews 11 candidates to replace Ukraine’s commander-in-chief Oleksandr Syrsky, Bloomberg reports
corpus.signal_text:
Zelensky reviews 11 candidates to replace Ukraine’s commander-in-chief Oleksandr Syrsky, Bloomberg reports

Volodymyr Zelensky is reviewing a list of 11 candidates to replace Oleksandr Syrsky as commander-in-chief of Ukraine’s armed forces, Bloomberg sources reported.

corpus.title: Protests over dismissal of Ukraine’s defense minister continue over weekend
corpus.signal_text:
Protests over dismissal of Ukraine’s defense minister continue over weekend

Hundreds of Kyiv residents gathered again outside the Ivan Franko Theater on the evening of July 19 to protest the dismissal of Mykhailo Fedorov as defense minister.

corpus.title: Zelenskyy's Dismissal Of Defense Minister Fedorov Sparks Protests
corpus.signal_text:
Zelenskyy's Dismissal Of Defense Minister Fedorov Sparks Protests

President Volodymyr Zelenskyy sacked Mykhaylo Fedorov as defense minister, a dramatic shakeup of his Cabinet that sent shockwaves through Ukrainian political and military circles and stoked new questions about oversight of Ukraine's stubborn defense against Russia's all-out invasion.

corpus.title: 'What A Nightmare': Zelenskyy's Dismissal Of Defense Minister Sparks Fury In Ukraine
corpus.signal_text:
'What A Nightmare': Zelenskyy's Dismissal Of Defense Minister Sparks Fury In Ukraine

Ukrainians reacted with fury to the abrupt ouster of Mykhaylo Fedorov, the tech-minded defense minister whose embrace of drone warfare innovation for many signaled a break from old-school military thinking and shady business interests.

corpus.title: Ukraine’s Zelensky meets commanders as street protests mount
corpus.signal_text:
Ukraine’s Zelensky meets commanders as street protests mount

Zelensky met five generals and one colonel, viewed by political analysts as potential replacements for his unpopular commander-in-chief.

corpus.title: Protests against Fedorov's dismissal continued for fifth day in Kyiv and other cities – photos
corpus.signal_text:
Protests against Fedorov's dismissal continued for fifth day in Kyiv and other cities – photos

For the fifth day running, people in Kyiv and other Ukrainian cities have taken to the streets to protest against the dismissal of former defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov and to call for the resignation of Oleksandr Syrskyi, Commander-in-Chief of the Armed Forces of Ukraine.

The Cabinet of Ministers has appointed Yevhen Khmara as acting defence minister.

corpus.title: Protests continue for fifth consecutive day in Kyiv and other cities
corpus.signal_text:
Protests continue for fifth consecutive day in Kyiv and other cities

In the center of the capital, on the square near the Franko Theater, as well as in other cities across Ukraine, rallies against the dismissal of Mykhailo Fedorov as Ukraine’s defense minister have been taking place for the fifth consecutive day.

corpus.title: As of today, Khmara is officially serving as acting DM
corpus.signal_text:
As of today, Khmara is officially serving as acting DM

As of today, July 20, Yevhen Khmara is officially serving as acting Minister of Defense of Ukraine.
